reviews.text:
I chose to have my fertility treatment with ARGC because their published statistics looked incredible. Unfortunately, my experience did not reflect the same standard.

The clinic can be quite difficult to navigate at first, though over time you do find your feet. My first visit was for immune testing — I had even called twice beforehand to confirm the details. However, when I arrived, I was mistakenly treated as if I was there for a monitoring cycle. After some confusion and back-and-forth, I was redirected to another clinic for the correct tests.

When my results came back showing elevated NK cells, I was understandably upset. Dr. Nawal was kind, clear, and supportive, explaining the next steps thoroughly. I later decided to proceed with a TSI (timed intercourse) round with Letrozole.

While the doctors I encountered were excellent — knowledgeable, empathetic, and concise — the communication with the nursing and administrative teams was consistently poor. Messages and emails often went unanswered, sometimes for days, which during treatment can feel like an eternity. I frequently had to repeat information that was already in my file and chase updates that should have been proactively shared.

During my cycle, there were repeated issues with clarity about next steps, medication requirements, and blood testing. On one occasion, I was contacted after leaving the clinic to return and give more blood due to a sample issue. Later, I discovered I had been given insufficient progesterone pessaries for my two-week wait, despite the team knowing I would be abroad. When I reached out for help, the responses were dismissive and lacked accountability.

I eventually submitted an official complaint after returning from treatment abroad. The response I received was defensive, inaccurate in parts, and did not acknowledge the stress caused by their poor communication and administration.

When my treatment ultimately did not work, I still reached out with openness to continue. Sadly, my final interaction — a phone call with the head nurse — was met with hostility and no compassion, despite just having shared the news that my treatment had failed.

The doctors at ARGC are undeniably skilled, and I have full respect for their medical expertise. However, the administrative, nursing, and patient care teams seriously undermine the clinic’s reputation. The lack of communication, empathy, and accountability made what was already an emotionally and financially challenging process far more stressful than it needed to be.

If you are considering ARGC, I would advise going in with caution — their doctors are excellent, but their support systems for patients fall significantly short of the standard you would expect from a leading fertility clinic.

I am sad to leave this review after many years at ARGC. I will start by saying that the admin staff and nurses are amazing. Unfortunately the doctors are so disorganised and frantic that no one knows who you are ever, and this leads to serious problems. ARGC put my health completely at risk (then joked about it) and I would really urge everyone to avoid the clinic for this reason. I lodged a complaint with them but there was no response of course

My experience of IVF treatment with ARGC was disappointing. While the clinic is known for high success rates, I now believe this may be partly due to their aggressive approach rather than careful practices like PGT-A testing—such as …
Awful - there is very poor communication and management of patients' mental health.  It is an enterprise designed to make a profit from women and family's desperation and misery.

Do dot use this clinic for IVF! Their treatments are extreme and dangerous. My wife was recommended unnecessary procedures at the wrong time which were evidently only being suggested so that could charge us more money. She was not warned of …

ARGC Malpractice Risked My Sister’s Life:

Unfortunately, my sister received treatment at
ARGC for an in vitro fertilization procedure. Despite the protocols indicating that implantation should be avoided if there are signs of ovarian hyper-stimulation, the clinic advised her to proceed with the implantation of two embryos. As a result, she developed ascites, renal failure, and severe electrolyte imbalances, putting her life at risk.

From the onset of the first symptoms, including difficulty breathing, abdominal swelling, nausea, and intolerance to food, my sister visited the clinic multiple times. However, no additional tests were performed, and her condition was downplayed. She was simply advised to increase fluid intake.

Fortunately, my sister went to A&E, where she was attended by the critical care team. They confirmed that her condition was life-threatening. The hospital later confirmed that she was the only patient they had treated with ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) who had embryos implanted. As no reputable clinic would recommend implantation when the ovaries are hyperstimulated.

Currently, she is hospitalized in critical condition due to complications arising from malpractice at ARGC, which failed to apply proper preventive measures and dismissed her initial symptoms, allowing her condition to worsen and putting her life in danger.

Please carefully consider your health and well-being before choosing ARGC for your fertility treatment.

Would leave a zero for the service and patient care if I could. They are involved in a purely money making enterprise with no thought or consideration for the women and families that come to them. Their so called service borders on …

Simply a negative experience through and through. Communication and environment are terrible, egg collection room looks very dodgy and not hygenic, getting information during scans is impossible- they almost can’t be bothered to talk to you, arrogance and entitlement of the doctors starting with Dr Taranissi is unbelievable.. Such high cost for such poor experience and results. It simply is not worth it. Although they have the reputation of ‘delivering results’ I think it is either an old or exaggerated story… Noone was able to provide any data.. Don’t waste your time and money and think that the clinic being on Harley St makes it good.

Have been researching IVF options in London for a family member - who will definitely not be using ARGC. Reasons to avoid: CEO Dr Taranassi is worth £30m+ but the clinic cuts costs by using eCourier, a company that pays workers less than minimum wage. (Don't assume savings are passed on - a single blood test costs £2,200 at ARGC.)

We had a demanding but overall acceptable experience all the way through egg collection. The problem is after having 10 eggs and 10 embryos fertilized on day 1, there was no check between day 1 and day 5. We were told on day 7 that we had none left. Nobody could explain to us what went wrong - just to try another cycle. Personally, it felt like we were constantly pushed to spend more on tests, and since we didn’t plan to transfer, perhaps they thought we were a bad stat anyway. Time and money wasted. More importantly, it took my husband and I four months to recover from that experience and now need to start over somewhere else.

I have am looking into fertility clinics at the moment, and was quite disgusted to see that ARGC uses Ecourier to transport essential medical samples. I can only assume that you are unaware that ECourier is pays their staff below minimum …

I'm really very surprised by any of the positive reviews I've read on this site. Our experience at ARGC was so awful. Someone wrote here that it feels like they are selling hope. That's exactly how we found it. The place is hugely overcrowded and so so shabby. We were ushered in from one test to the next with little or no explantation of what it was all for. Each time we went through the door we paid more money and we didn't know why.  After the first consultation we didn't have any connection with the doctor apart from one not very pleasant scan where I asked him what my results meant. He didn't look at me and said that I had a very low chance of getting pregnant and that was it. I was devastated and left the room.

After having spent over £1000 on bloodtests over several weeks we went in to start the treatment. On this day we found out that they needed another £5000 for medication on top of the £5000 we knew about. We hadn't been told about this and when we complained they said we should have asked about how much it was going to cost - which we had done repeatedly. The price list we had been given was very obtuse and hadn't made this remotely clear. I was devastated. We didn't have this money set aside. It felt like our chance of having a baby was taken away from us and we'd thrown away the money we'd spent so far.

Some of the staff were so uncaring. You felt like you were wasting their time with a question. I felt like they just wanted our money. There was no human connection. Yes they get good results for people who are able to pay for it. But that can't be all there is. You should feel cared for and comforted and kept in the light when going through something as potentially as stressful as IVF.

But the good news is we found a clinic in the Czech Republic - in Prague called UNICA. The clinic was clean and calm with a wonderful zen like atmosphere. The doctors available to talk and tell you about your treatment at every step. The and receptionists who know your name and ask how you are. Above all you have a dedicated co-ordinator who answers any questions you have, looks after you, walks in with you before the operation and there for you at the end. She tells you what drugs you are taking and why. She gives you a little pep talk when you need it and reminds you to be realistic when you need that too. In short it was the best care we could have hoped for. For this exceptional treatment, scans and all medication, flights, accommodation, food, concerts in the most beautiful of cities and a holiday of a lifetime the whole thing came to £5000 - about £7000 less than we would have paid at ARGC. We don't yet know whether we are pregnant but the experience was magical and I'd urge anyone thinking about doing IVF to explore options abroad.

The lack of follow up after a traumatic round of treatment which ended up with the hospitalisation of my wife, who had severe OHSS from an egg collection, is disgusting. Complete negligence from the staff at the time - they should have …

ARGC is one of the worst clinics in the U.K. I froze my eggs with them in 2016, got 19 in total, I thought I had preserved my fertility. However in 2020, when I wanted to use my frozen eggs, Dr Sapna continued to insist we should do a fresh cycle, which made no sense to given we I had eggs saved from when I was much younger. Normally When you thaw eggs, survival rate is 90%- ARGC lost 17/19eggs while thawing them.

I have take two opinions since, one from a clinic in the US and one at St Mary’s hospital, both confirmed that their lab must be sub standard and embryologist are not trained properly given our outcome.

We requested a full investigation, which came back with “we followed protocol on the day”. After such a disastrous outcome- Dr Taranassi never spoke with us or emailed us ( even though we requested it). Please do yourself a favour and pick a clinic with modern lab like Care Fertility/CRGH or anyone else really. Absolutely AVOID ARGC- substandard is the only description.

Paid them a deposit last year for an appointment that never happened due to lack of information (not) communicated by staff at ARGC. Was told I'd get a refund but am still waiting on the money. Very disappointing.

Months ago we started treatment here. Told us it will cost around £6000-£8000. At the end we paid £15,000 for nothing. Had a Miscarriage.  Some of the staff really kind. Few of them are so rude. Doctors are very kind and helpful. Everything …

I abhorred my experience there:
- Paper printed medical records
- Dated decor, blue carpet and dusty fake flowers
- Conflicting information between the scanning nurse and the nurses
- Super rushed atmosphere at the time when you as a patient are the most vulnerable
- Zero to none consultant time
- Zero to non consideration for the patient time and wallet
- They will insist you have milk while you are in stimulation phase, I am vegetarian and follow a lactose free diet. There is no scientific ground for recommending this.

Would not go back there even if I was gifted another chance...

Terrible experience! Trust me when I say, do not use this company. Communication is terrible, service is terrible, constantly being told different things by different people. At so many occasions we were let down. We are currently in the process of communicating with the ombudsman. Please beware

They treat women like money-making objects. If you are sick, they do not show any concern. They just tell you to call NHS! So much for private clinic. They don’t even have an elevator inside. What if you feel sick and can’t walk? …

This is a factory. On the surface it appears to be tailored but this is mostly smoke and mirrors: They just follow their standard procedures. The details of the patient are really skipped over. Most of he nurses are pleasant and professional but the infrastructure, record keeping and patient care are from the 70s. You just get the feeling they want you to do cycle after cycle. $$$

I wish they treated people better. They make you feel like cattle. Why not spend some of the bags if money and invest in a computerised system with set appointment times and a decent waiting room so that women at the clinic are treated better.

I would give them zero stars if that is possible!
Total waste of time, do not listen to patient concerns, don’t listen to clients needs I don’t think they care much either! One nurse or staff member said one …

Very rushed, stressful, impersonal and too expensive.  After spending 12k I did not even have any eggs left over to freeze!!.  -ve result. When i approached the team seeking counselling as i was upset all they did was try to sell me further sessions!!!!! I left deeply upset. V bad experience.
I had very bad experience with them. One if the consultants didn’t write the consultation outcome correctly which resulted in incorrect timing of meds. Unfortunately this place is not for me

Very disorganised , too Manuel, no electronic confirmation . No fixed time for appointment . Have to wait around in the area for no necessary reason. Have to do 5 am injection which no other clinics make you do
Its a scam! I booked for an appointment 3 workdays ago, no one answers the phone. No calls received

Tribble experience, rude staff.  the worse thing they are really disorganise.
This is really sensitive and you need better clinic.  Worse experience ever!!

Commercial, they only care about the money I paid a lot of money and i never saw the doctor not even once, he didn’t have the dignity to give me the bad news, I repeatedly ask to see him and he refused.
Pseudo-scientific bordering reckless patient engagement at highly inflated prices.
Unfortunately they treat people terribly at this clinic
Avoid this place !

Disappointing.

The wait times and bedside manner are just as disappointing as the NHS and when I am spending hundreds of pounds for a service I expect better.  Most disappointingly, the doctor did not introduce herself nor say hello, when I walked in she just ordered me to undress and get on the chair. I get that the practice busy but empathy and conversational skills is apart of the job and not using them is not how you make someone feel comfortable before an internal pelvic scan.

I have also been emailing for a number of months requesting a copy of my ultrasound report to which I have had no response. I am hoping this review will escalate a response from your company.

I am still waiting for my treatment and might change my feedback later, but I have to tell that if a donor is required, you need to deal with “the lab”, which seems like a separate set up with little communication with nurses and doctors. They answer emails one a week, don’t communicate well about what’s required, and possible delaying my treatment with no concern whatsoever. Any enquiry is answered again one week later. Any respectable clinic can’t have a lab operating like this!

Terrible experience, despite high cost the clinic is extremely disorganised (my file couldn’t be found several times they had booked me in), terrible communication (nurses on the same day providing different information), lack of clarity on the full process and costs. There were consistent delays and you feel rushed at every step. It is managed like a production line. The experience has definitely not been positive and if I went back in time I would not choose ARGC

I recently went to the ARGC for an egg freezing procedure and initially had a very positive experience. Based on previous reviews I was expecting it to be fast-paced with less attention given to each patient, but most of the nurses were …

The treatment was very tailored, but despite that, my overall experience with ARGC is negative. They operate on very high volume of patients, staff look overloaded and the human element is missing. It is a stressful treatment and the busy, rushed atmosphere at ARGC makes the whole experience miserable.

The main issue is with how they treat people. You are just a number. You wait 30 min to 1 hour for a scan, they don’t try to speed things up or optimise, they don’t care. You never get privacy when speaking about your situation, it’s always in front of 5 other people waiting by the front desk. The morning of my egg collections, one of the nurses yelled at me because 1 page in my file was not filled in. The first day I collected the meds, no one had told me I would get a large bag full of meds which I had to take with me to a conference in Canary Wharf.
March 2020 update: the IVF with ARGC did not work, the process was hard physically and mentally. I then fell pregnant naturally 1 year later and now have a healthy and happy baby! So hopefully there is light at the end of the tunnel for those of you researching IVF.

I did not realize how expensive IVF here would be. I thought the £3300 IVF fee (which on my bill they insisted is £3380) would be the most expensive part but it’s the opposite! Before you even undergo any treatment you have to do …

We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the ARGC in Jan 2022.
IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims.
We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS consultant gave us a 3% chance of success.
My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (2nd with the Evewell) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!
